key_metrics_output: Quality assessment scores for effect studies (20 criteria across 4 categories: particle characterization, experimental design, applicability in risk assessment, ecological relevance); weight of evidence for effect mechanisms; effect thresholds (LCx, ECx, LOEC, NOEC)
abstract: This critical review assessed the quality of 105 microplastic effect studies with aquatic biota by developing 20 quality criteria across four main categories: particle characterization, experimental design, applicability in risk assessment, and ecological relevance. Studies scored an average of 44.6% of maximum quality score (range 20-77.5%), with no study meeting all criteria. Key findings indicate urgent need for improved background contamination control and environmentally relevant exposure conditions. While 86.7% of studies adequately characterized particles, a mismatch existed between tested and environmentally targeted particle sizes. Of 17 proposed effect mechanisms, only 3 remained well-supported when accounting for study quality: inhibition of food assimilation/decreased nutritional value, internal physical damage, and external physical damage. The review provides quality assessment methodology and guidance protocol for future microplastic effect studies to support risk assessment.

particle_size_range: Studies were only included when at least one type of MP tested had a diameter between 1 µm and 5 mm
